reason: All three Phase 2 deliverables are internally consistent and cross-traceable. The 4-module decomposition (cli/store/executor/config) in SAD §2 matches the SAB `layers` and `allowed_dependencies` blocks; every `fr_module_traceability` entry points to a real module (FR-01→store, FR-02→executor, FR-03→cli); NFR targets are measurable (p95<50ms for NFR-01, ≥95 coverage-floor with explicit metric for NFR-02/NFR-03). ADR-008's '45 inventory names are binding' rule is honored by TEST_SPEC.md (45/45 inventory names preserved verbatim; 15 additional gap-filled names derived via documented Q1–Q8 protocol). All 3 FRs (FR-01, FR-02, FR-03) have ≥1 dedicated TEST_SPEC section plus cross-cutting coverage. Terminology (atomic_write, shell=False, StoreCorrupted, exit codes 0/2/4/1, TASKQ_HOME/TASKQ_TASK_TIMEOUT/TASKQ_RETRY_LIMIT, redact/redaction) is consistent across all three docs. The Phase 2 sub-task round-2 APPROVE status (SAD, ADR, TEST_SPEC) is reflected throughout. Gaps are cosmetic only.
citations: SAD.md §1 (4-module decomposition; store is spine, executor riskiest)
SAD.md §2.1 (CRG Principles 1–6; hub-and-spoke per directory; Principle 4 hub-call coverage table)
SAD.md §2.2.1–2.2.4 (module specs; hub modules format.py / persistence.py / runner.py / env.py)
SAD.md §3.1–3.4 (data flows; submit/run/timeout+retry/corruption)
SAD.md §4 (Error Handling table; anti-silencing rule)
SAD.md §5 (SAB block: 4 layers, 7 allowed_dependency edges, fr_module_traceability, nfr_traceability with measurable targets)
SAD.md §6 (stdlib inventory: subprocess, shlex, json, os.replace, uuid, argparse, pathlib, re, datetime, time, dataclasses, sys)
SAD.md §7 (25-test cross-reference table; 25-vs-45 reconciliation note)
ADR.md ADR-001 (stdlib-only; no third-party runtime deps; static-scan test as enforcement)
ADR.md ADR-002 (4-module DAG: cli→{store,executor,config}, executor→{store,config}, store→{config}, config→{})
ADR.md ADR-003 (subprocess.run + shell=False + shlex.split; defense-in-depth pair with FR-01 blacklist)
ADR.md ADR-004 (tmp + os.replace + fsync; no silent rebuild on corruption)
ADR.md ADR-005 (4-state FSM; strict transition table; retry as repeated running→{failed,timeout}→running cycles)
ADR.md ADR-006 (redaction at executor boundary; sk-[A-Za-z0-9_-]{8,} | token=\S+ patterns)
ADR.md ADR-007 (4-code mapping 0/2/4/1; anti-silencing rule; no bare except)
ADR.md ADR-008 (P1 inventory naming authority; 45/45 inventory preserved)
TEST_SPEC.md NFR Pattern Activation table (NP-04, NP-06, NP-08, NP-10, NP-13 forced by SAD, NP-15 forced by SAD)
TEST_SPEC.md FR-01 12 cases (validation/id/atomic/corruption/concurrent/round-trip/no-silent-rebuild)
TEST_SPEC.md FR-02 19 cases (state machine valid/invalid transitions, shell=False, timeout, retry cap, tail caps, duration/finished_at, orphan cleanup, no-swallow)
TEST_SPEC.md FR-03 11 cases (subcommand happy paths, unknown id, json flag, help text, list truncation, exit code matrix, E2E, no-zero-on-validation)
TEST_SPEC.md Cross-Cutting NFR Integration 17 cases (p95 KPI, redteam static scan, redaction unit+integration, atomic-write SIGKILL+concurrent, env keys/defaults)
TEST_SPEC.md Deployment Smoke 1 case (--help returns 0)
TEST_SPEC.md Summary table (60 total = 45 inventory verbatim + 15 gap-filled via Q1–Q8; 7 NFR patterns active; sum check 60 ✓)

gaps.message: TEST_SPEC.md FR-02 case table numbering jumps from #17 directly to #19 (missing #18). Row count (19) and summary metric match the actual entries, but the #18 gap is a cosmetic inconsistency that could confuse reviewers scanning row numbers.

SAD.md §3.2 contains a syntactic fragment: 'store.update_task(id, status="done" written)   # second atomic_write'. Looks like an editing artifact; the second atomic_write call should be either a complete store.update_task(id, status="done") call or a labelled comment. No semantic impact (already described above as 'second atomic_write').

SAB `nfr_traceability.NFR-02` and `NFR-03` use target value '>=95' with the comment 'raise gate floor: ...'. The metric is implicit (test-coverage percentage) and the threshold is given but not the exact formula (e.g. '5/6 injection chars covered = 83% — would fail the floor'). For a binding contract, an explicit metric name (e.g. 'coverage_pct') would remove ambiguity. Still measurable, still above the documented metric floor when 6/6 + atomic-write + redaction all pass.

SAD.md NFR-03 SAB module assignment is `taskq.store` ('atomic write lives in store/persistence.py; redaction enforced via store.update_task contract'), but ADR-006 places the redaction *implementation* in `taskq.executor.redaction`. This is a deliberate primary-surface assignment (the store is the contract enforcement point), but a reader cross-checking ADR-006 against SAB may need the dual-module note to understand the split. Worth a one-line clarification in the SAB `nfr_traceability.NFR-03` module comment.

Quality manifest (.methodology/quality_manifest.json) is referenced in the holistic checklist but was not included in the provided documents. Cannot verify from the supplied context that all 3 FRs are present in the manifest. Recommendation: a separate verification pass against the manifest file by the integrator before Gate-2 close.

TEST_SPEC.md summary line 'TEST_INVENTORY.yaml names preserved: 100% — 45/45 inventory function names appear verbatim' could be cross-verified mechanically (diff TEST_SPEC test-function names against TEST_INVENTORY.yaml v1.1). The summary asserts preservation; a 5-line diff script in `harness/check_test_spec_inventory.py` would make this check CI-enforceable rather than claim-based.
